summary: Obfuscated payload with PowerShell execution at install time
details: Package ships a heavily obfuscated server.obf.js (variable names like _0x54368a, _0x4ecc0b, _0x53e6a2 — typical hex-name obfuscation) alongside launcher.js, which invokes execSync with PowerShell commands at multiple call sites (lines 131, 142, 151, 246). The combination of obfuscated code plus repeated PowerShell shell-out from a launcher script is the canonical Windows-targeted dropper/loader shape: obfuscation hides intent from review, PowerShell is the execution surface for fetching and running additional payloads or harvesting host data. The package name (sensivity) carries no documented legitimate purpose that would justify executing PowerShell on the installer's machine, and there is no evident reason for a Node module to need spawn-PowerShell-from-obfuscated-loader behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
